module.title: Week 24: Network Forensics & PCAP Analysis
module.focus: Wireshark, Zeek, PCAP analysis, network detection

topics.title: Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Master Wireshark for security analysis
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Analyze a pcap file using Wireshark display filters
topics.interviewQuestion: What are the key Wireshark interface features?
topics.interviewAnswer: Packet list (color-coded by protocol), packet details (expandable protocol tree), and packet bytes (hex dump). Display filters use protocol-specific fields like http.request, dns.qry.name, tcp.port==443. Features: Follow TCP/UDP streams, Statistics menu, TLS decryption, and file extraction.

quiz.question: What color-coding does Wireshark use?
What does 'Follow TCP Stream' do?
What menu shows protocol hierarchy?
What can Wireshark decrypt?
What did the ransomware Wireshark investigation show?
options: SMB write spike at encryption time

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark is a foundational cybersecurity concept that defines how systems work. It is a security principle, not a programming language or protocol.

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark?
quiz.explanation: Security principles like those in Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark typically span confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

quiz.question: Where does Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark apply in real-world scenarios?
quiz.explanation: Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark concepts are universal across all security domains.

quiz.question: Which certification exam covers Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark?
quiz.explanation: Most major certifications touch on Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark at some level.

quiz.question: Best way to gain hands-on experience with Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark?
quiz.explanation: Practical application in a home lab is the most effective way to build skills. Reading alone is not enough.

quiz.question: Which skill is NOT directly related to Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark?
quiz.explanation: While web development may use security concepts, Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark is more directly tied to risk, response, and monitoring.

quiz.question: Scenario: You encounter Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark during a security audit. What is your first step?
quiz.explanation: A professional approach to Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark during an audit is to identify the issue and document findings for further analysis.

quiz.question: What is a common tool used with Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark?
quiz.explanation: Depending on context, Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark can involve firewalls, SIEMs, or packet analyzers.

quiz.question: Understanding Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark helps prevent which type of attack?
quiz.explanation: The specific attack Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark prevents depends on its context within the security domain.

quiz.question: How does Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark relate to the CIA triad?
quiz.explanation: Most security concepts including Day 1: Network Traffic Analysis with Wireshark can impact all three pillars of the CIA triad.

topics.title: Day 2: Wireshark Deep Dive: Filters & Analysis
topics.description: Use advanced Wireshark filters for threat hunting
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 2: Wireshark Deep Dive: Filters & Analysis is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Use advanced Wireshark filters for threat hunting
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Write complex display filters to find C2 beaconing and data exfiltration
topics.interviewQuestion: What is Wireshark filter syntax and capabilities?
topics.interviewAnswer: Comparison operators (eq, ne, gt, lt), logical (and, or, not), contains, matches (regex), and bitwise operations. Filters can chain conditions: (ip.src==10.0.0.1 and tcp.port==443) or dns.qry.name matches '\.(exe|dll)$'. Macros save commonly used filters.

quiz.question: What operator checks regex in Wireshark?
What bitwise operation can filters do?
What C2 beacon fingerprint can be detected?
options: SYN packets with small window sizes

quiz.question: What saves commonly used filters?
What does coloring rules do?

topics.title: Day 3: Zeek Network Security Monitor
topics.description: Use Zeek for connection-based network analysis
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 3: Zeek Network Security Monitor is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Use Zeek for connection-based network analysis
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Install Zeek and analyze its structured log output
topics.interviewQuestion: How does Zeek differ from Wireshark?
topics.interviewAnswer: Zeek is connection-centric, producing structured logs (conn.log, dns.log, http.log, ssl.log, files.log) ideal for SIEM ingestion. Wireshark is packet-centric for real-time analysis. Zeek scripts extend functionality for custom detections.

quiz.question: What does conn.log contain?
options: Connection summaries with duration, bytes, protocols

quiz.question: What does dns.log contain?
What does notice.log contain?
What does files.log contain?
How did the university use Zeek during ransomware?
options: Queried conn.log for pre-encryption SMB connections

topics.title: Day 4: PCAP Analysis & Forensics
topics.description: Perform forensic analysis of packet captures
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 4: PCAP Analysis & Forensics is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Perform forensic analysis of packet captures
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Analyze a malware-traffic-analysis.net pcap and write an incident report
topics.interviewQuestion: What is the PCAP analysis methodology?
topics.interviewAnswer: Overview (IO graph, protocol hierarchy), Filter (isolate suspicious traffic), Extract (pull files, credentials, artifacts), Timeline (reconstruct events via TCP streams), and Report (document findings with screenshots). Tools: tshark, NetworkMiner, Brim/Zui.

quiz.question: What is step 1 of PCAP analysis?
options: Overview - examine IO graph and protocol hierarchy

quiz.question: What is step 2?
What is NetworkMiner used for?
What does Brim/Zui provide?
What was exfiltrated in the law firm breach?
options: ZIP file of legal documents via HTTP POST

topics.title: Day 5: Network Detection Rules & Anomaly Detection Project
topics.content:
> **Why This Gets You Hired:** Mastering Day 5: Network Detection Rules & Anomaly Detection Project is a must-have for any cybersecurity role. SOC analysts use this daily, penetration testers build on it, and hiring managers specifically look for this skill on resumes.

:::objectives
- Build network-based detection rules
:::
topics.aiPrompt: Implement beaconing, data exfiltration, and brute force network detection rules
topics.interviewQuestion: What are network detection rule types?
topics.interviewAnswer: Signature-based (known bad patterns like C2 fingerprints), behavioral (traffic volume thresholds, connection frequency), and statistical (deviation from baseline). Common: beaconing detection, data exfiltration (large outbound transfers), DNS tunneling, and protocol abuse.

quiz.question: What does signature-based detection match?
options: Known bad patterns like C2 fingerprints

quiz.question: What does beaconing detection look for?
options: Periodic connections to the same IP at regular intervals

quiz.question: What is DNS tunneling?
options: Abnormal DNS query sizes or frequencies

quiz.question: What is protocol abuse?
What did the SOC detect with Zeek brute force rules?
options: SSH brute force on bastion host

quiz.question: Which best describes Day 5: Network Detection Rules & Anomaly Detection Project?
